prompt: Map the distillation / fine-tuning-as-a-service and 'own your model' landscape in 2026 (OpenPipe, Predibase, distil labs, Together, Fireworks, Baseten, Modal): players, funding, what's commoditized, and the gaps. Cite sources.
content:
**In 2026, the distillation/fine-tuning-as-a-service (FTaaS) and "own your model" landscape centers on managed platforms that let developers and enterprises replace expensive proprietary LLM calls (e.g., GPT-4) with cheaper, faster, task-specific open-source or distilled small language models (SLMs).** This is driven by cost savings (often 50-90%+ on inference), performance gains on specialized tasks via fine-tuning or distillation (synthetic data from larger "teacher" models), and desires for data control/privacy.[[1]](https://sacra.com/research/kyle-corbitt-openpipe-fine-tuning-llms/)[[2]](https://www.distillabs.ai/)

Key trends include:
- **Open-source model proliferation** enabling specialization.
- **Managed services abstracting complexity** (data collection/logging, training, eval, deployment, serving).
- **Consolidation via acquisitions** (e.g., OpenPipe by CoreWeave; Predibase by Rubrik).
- **Heavy VC backing** for inference/FT platforms, with NVIDIA ecosystem ties (Blackwell optimizations driving 10x cost/token reductions for several players).
- **Market growth**: FTaaS projected to expand rapidly (one estimate: from ~$3.8B in 2025 to $28.6B by 2034 at 25%+ CAGR), though hyperscalers and specialists compete.[[3]](https://dataintelo.com/report/fine-tuning-as-a-service-market)

Players like OpenPipe, Predibase, distil labs focus more on fine-tuning/distillation workflows; Together AI, Fireworks AI, Baseten, and Modal emphasize high-performance inference with FT capabilities layered on top. "Own your model" emphasizes hosted or deployable custom weights (vs. black-box APIs), often with per-token or dedicated pricing.

### Key Players, Offerings, and Funding (as of mid-2026)
- **OpenPipe**: Developer-first SFT/RL fine-tuning platform; logs prompts/completions automatically, trains specialized models (often distilling from larger ones like GPT-4), deploys cheaper/faster alternatives. Emphasizes continuous iteration for product teams. Acquired by CoreWeave (~2025); founder Kyle Corbitt now leads serverless training there. Seed: $6.7M (2024, Costanoa + YC + angels).[[4]](https://openpipe.ai/blog/announcing-6-7m-seed-raise)[[5]](https://www.cognitiverevolution.ai/the-rl-fine-tuning-playbook-coreweave-s-kyle-corbitt-on-grpo-rubrics-environments-reward-hacking/)[[6]](https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/openpipe)

- **Predibase**: Low-code/enterprise FT platform for open-source LLMs; supports specialized AI, reinforcement fine-tuning (RFT), efficient serving (flat per-token pricing, e.g., Llama-2-7B at low cost). Fine-Tuning Index/leaderboards from experiments. Acquired by Rubrik (2025, reported $100M+). Total funding: ~$28.5M (Series A expanded, Felicis/Greylock-led).[[7]](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20230531005393/en/Predibase-Empowers-Any-Engineer-to-Build-Their-Own-GPT-With-Support-for-Large-Language-Models-Expands-Series-A-Funding-to-%2428M)[[8]](https://siliconangle.com/2025/06/25/rubrik-acquires-llm-tooling-startup-predibase-reported-100m/)

- **distil labs** (distillabs.ai): Emerging specialist in automated distillation/SLM creation. Ingests production traces, generates synthetic data, fine-tunes custom small models (no labeling needed; CLI/tools for quick training/deployment). Claims 50-90% inference cost cuts; focuses on replacing LLMs with task-specific SLMs. Limited public funding details (appears bootstrapped/early-stage as of 2026).[[2]](https://www.distillabs.ai/)[[9]](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/jacek-golebiowski_today-were-shipping-the-distil-labs-claude-activity-7453093921871581185-sEIm)

- **Together AI**: Full-stack "AI acceleration cloud" — broad open-source model catalog, serverless/batch/dedicated inference (OpenAI-compatible), LoRA + full fine-tuning, pre-training support. Strong throughput and customization. Significant scale (high ARR). Funding: $305M Series B (2025, $3.3B val, General Catalyst-led); later ~$1B raise talks at $7.5B–$8.5B val (2026). Total prior ~$537M+.[[10]](https://www.together.ai/blog/together-ai-announcing-305m-series-b)[[11]](https://www.pminsights.com/companies/together-ai)

- **Fireworks AI**: High-speed inference optimized for open-source models (including vision/agents); fine-tuning via Multi-LoRA, RFT, and "Refinery" for sub-billion parameter distilled models. Global scale, partnerships (e.g., Microsoft Foundry/Azure). Funding: $250M Series C (Oct 2025, $4B val, Lightspeed/Index/Evantic-led + Sequoia/NVIDIA/etc.); earlier rounds to ~$327M total. Later higher-val talks.[[12]](https://fireworks.ai/blog/series-c)[[13]](https://sacra.com/c/fireworks-ai/)

- **Baseten**: Production inference platform for custom/fine-tuned/open-source models; dedicated endpoints, observability, SLAs, horizontal scale. Added training SDK (e.g., Loops for frontier RL). Strong in performant, reliable serving. Funding: Multiple rounds including $150M Series D (Sep 2025, $2.15B val); $300M Series E (Jan 2026, $5B val, IVP/CapitalG/NVIDIA anchor). Later $1B raise talks at ~$11B val. High revenue growth.[[14]](https://www.baseten.co/blog/announcing-baseten-150m-series-d/)[[15]](https://sacra.com/c/baseten/)

- **Modal**: Code-first/Python SDK serverless platform for inference, training, batch jobs, custom containers, and ML workflows (not purely API-driven). Flexible for custom models and full pipelines; burst capacity. Funding details sparse; mentions of valuation talks (~$2.5B range in context of peers).[[16]](https://www.digitalocean.com/resources/articles/ai-inference-platforms)

### What's Commoditized
- **Core inference serving** on popular open-weight models (per-token pricing, OpenAI-compatible endpoints, basic optimizations like quantization/continuous batching). Performance clusters across providers (e.g., Fireworks, Together, Baseten).[[17]](https://eastwind.substack.com/p/a-deep-dive-on-ai-inference-startups)
- **Basic fine-tuning** (especially LoRA/adapters) and serving of fine-tuned models on the same platform.
- **Synthetic data generation** and simple distillation pipelines (increasingly automated; overlaps with FT workflows).
- **Cost/performance baselines** via NVIDIA Blackwell and similar hardware-software co-design (multiple providers claiming up to 10x token cost reductions).[[18]](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/inference-open-source-models-blackwell-reduce-cost-per-token/)
- **Managed hosting/deployment** for custom weights ("own your model" at scale without self-managing GPUs).

These elements have matured into table-stakes offerings, with differentiation shifting to speed, price, integrations, or advanced features.

### Gaps and Opportunities
- **Advanced/RL fine-tuning and quality iteration**: While Predibase and OpenPipe/CoreWeave push RFT/RL, seamless tools for non-experts to achieve frontier-level specialized performance (with robust eval/reward modeling) remain emerging.[[19]](https://nextword.substack.com/p/finetuning-llms-for-enterprises-interview)
- **Enterprise-grade governance, safety, and compliance**: Handling risks like harmful fine-tuning attacks (which can jailbreak aligned models even with benign data); auditability, data residency, and on-prem/hybrid options lag behind pure cloud FTaaS.[[20]](https://arxiv.org/html/2409.18169v6)
- **End-to-end for product teams (vs. ML experts)**: Better abstraction of data collection, versioning, A/B testing, and observability loops; full "distill once, own forever" with minimal ops.
- **Tiny/specialized SLMs at extreme scale**: Automated distillation to sub-billion or edge-viable models with guaranteed quality parity on narrow tasks (distil labs targets this, but broader adoption and benchmarking needed).
- **Seamless multi-provider or portable "own your model"**: Avoiding lock-in while retaining high performance; better support for proprietary teacher model distillation (legal/ToS hurdles persist).[[21]](https://www.facebook.com/techcrunch/posts/distillation-is-a-hot-topic-as-frontier-labs-try-to-prevent-smaller-competitors-/1317959083531289/)
- **Integration depth**: Tighter MLOps/compound AI workflows (agents, RAG, vision) beyond core FT/inference.

Overall, the space is consolidating around well-funded inference giants (Together, Fireworks, Baseten) with FT add-ons, while specialists (OpenPipe ecosystem, Predibase via acquirer, distil labs) target distillation/FT workflows. Expect further M&A, NVIDIA influence, and focus on RL/safety as open models close the gap with proprietary ones. Sources primarily from company announcements, funding reports, and industry analyses (2024–2026).
